notes: Windows + Cursor CLI (agent) + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Remove '摘要配置' label above summary profile selector in both summary entry paths
userStories.description: In article summary pages reached from both RSS subscriptions and article favorites entry paths, remove the '摘要配置' text shown above the summary-profile dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: When entering article summary from RSS subscriptions path, the text '摘要配置' above the dropdown is removed.
When entering article summary from article favorites path, the text '摘要配置' above the dropdown is removed.
Summary profile selector remains visible and fully functional in both paths.
No other summary page labels or controls are unintentionally changed.
Related frontend tests are updated and pass for both entry paths.

userStories.title: Fix RSS subscriptions page title placement
userStories.description: Correct RSS subscriptions page title rendering by using the standard page title area (matching article favorites page) and removing duplicated text above add-feed controls.
acceptanceCriteria: On RSS subscriptions page, the page title displays as 'RSS 订阅' in the standard page title location consistent with article favorites page.
The extra 'RSS 订阅' text currently shown above the '添加 Feed' control is removed.
No other controls or layout on RSS subscriptions page are unintentionally changed.
Relevant frontend tests are updated or added, and pass.

userStories.title: Unify article delete confirmation dialog in favorites article list
userStories.description: In article favorites -> article list, replace the article deletion confirmation UI with the same standardized delete confirmation dialog used on other pages.
acceptanceCriteria: In article favorites -> article list, clicking delete on an article opens the standardized delete confirmation dialog used on other pages.
Deletion confirmation dialog in article list matches existing app-wide delete confirmation UI behavior and visual style.
Confirmed deletion still removes the target article correctly; cancel action leaves the article unchanged.
Relevant frontend tests are updated or added, and pass.

userStories.title: Add read-later collection data file with reference-only storage
userStories.description: Create read-later collection data in data directory using reference-only records (no duplicated article/summary entity), ensuring DRY data design.
acceptanceCriteria: A new read-later data file exists under data directory (agreed filename: data/read_later.json).
Read-later records store only article references/identifiers and metadata needed for ordering, without duplicating article content or summary content.
Adding an article to read-later inserts a new record; removing it deletes the corresponding record.
Read-later ordering is newest-added first.
No changes introduce separate persisted article or summary entities for read-later.

userStories.title: Add read-later toggle button on article summary page from RSS subscriptions
userStories.description: On article summary page entered from RSS subscriptions path, add a read-later toggle button to the right of article title with plus/minus icon and stateful red style when already in read-later.
acceptanceCriteria: On RSS subscriptions -> article summary page, a read-later button is shown to the right of article title.
If current article is not in read-later, button displays plus icon with text '待读'.
Clicking plus-state button adds the article into read-later collection and updates button to red minus-state immediately.
If current article is already in read-later, button displays red minus icon with text '待读'.
Clicking minus-state button removes the article from read-later collection and updates button back to plus-state.

userStories.title: Add read-later toggle button on article summary page from article favorites
userStories.description: On article summary page entered from article favorites path, add the same read-later toggle behavior and visual states as RSS path.
acceptanceCriteria: On article favorites -> article summary page, a read-later button is shown to the right of article title.
Button states and behaviors are identical to RSS path: plus add, red minus remove.
Read-later records for article favorites are treated as independent entries from RSS-origin articles (no cross-source equivalence).
State rendering is correct after page reload/navigation.
No existing summary controls are unintentionally changed.

userStories.title: Add read-later panel on home page under RSS subscriptions and article favorites
userStories.description: Add a new home page panel named '待读' below RSS subscriptions and article favorites entry panels, showing all read-later article titles in newest-added-first order.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page contains a new panel titled '待读' placed below RSS subscriptions and article favorites entry panels.
Panel lists all read-later article titles with no truncation by count.
Displayed ordering is newest-added first.
When read-later is empty, panel shows a clear empty-state hint.
Panel data is sourced via read-later references and resolved from existing article data (no duplicated article entity).

userStories.title: Enable navigation from home read-later list to summary page with correct read-later state
userStories.description: Clicking any title in home read-later panel opens the corresponding article summary page and shows minus red read-later state by default.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking any read-later title on home page opens its corresponding article summary page.
Opened summary page renders read-later button in red minus-state by default for that article.
Removing from read-later on opened summary page updates home read-later list after returning/refreshing.
Navigation works for both RSS-origin and article-favorites-origin read-later entries.

userStories.title: Add and update frontend tests for read-later end-to-end flows
userStories.description: Add/update tests to cover read-later data behavior, summary page toggles (both entry paths), home read-later panel rendering, and navigation/state consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover add/remove read-later from RSS summary page.
Tests cover add/remove read-later from article favorites summary page.
Tests verify home read-later panel shows all items in newest-added-first order.
Tests verify navigation from read-later list to summary page and correct minus red-state rendering.
Relevant frontend tests pass.
